data.title: Hassett seems to confirm Trump administration is talking to AI firms about a possible stake
data.description:
National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett seemed to confirm that OpenAI (OPENAI) has proposed giving the U.S. government a 5% stake in the artificial intelligence company.

“We’re talking to all the big AI companies and seeing what they think about what they could do to share their upside with the American people,” Hassett said in a Thursday interview with CNBC. Hassett did not give further details on what the discussions entailed. 

However, Hassett said it was an “open question” of whether the U.S. federal government would take a stake in companies, similar to what  it did with Intel (INTC). He described the Intel stake as being driven by “unique” circumstances.

The Financial Times reported that OpenAI has discussed giving a 5% stake to the U.S. federal government as the $852B AI start-up seeks to clear political obstacles. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has argued that giving the public a financial stake in the company is the best way to share the upside of AI. 

Altman has been in active talks with the Trump administration about the issue of public ownership, including President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, according to multiple people familiar with the matter.

The proposed arrangement would involve other U.S. AI companies handing over a similar stake, although it is not clear if the other labs would be willing to do so, the report said.

Separately, on Thursday, Reuters reported that Anthropic (ANTHRO) has not spoken to the U.S. government about taking a financial stake in the Claude maker.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

(This story has been updated with news on Anthropic.)

data.title: BHP seeks permit for $1.5B restart of Cerro Colorado copper mine
data.description:
BHP (BHP) said late Wednesday it filed an environmental permit application for a $1.5B project to reopen the Cerro Colorado copper mine in Chile for an additional 20 years.

BHP (BHP) said it plans to use treated wastewater transported through a ~60-mile pipeline from the municipality of Alto Hospicio to the mine site.

The company closed the mine in 2023 but said it would explore options to restart operations, including through the use of leaching technologies and desalinated water.

Separately, BHP (BHP) said Thursday it agreed to sell its San Manuel property in Arizona to Faraday Copper (CPPKF) in exchange for shares equivalent to a 30% fully diluted interest in Faraday, raising its stake in the company to 32.5%.

The agreement provides a pathway for the development of a new copper hub in Arizona, combining existing infrastructure and mineral inventory at San Manuel with Faraday’s (CPPKF) adjacent Copper Creek project.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase teams up with Gulf banks on $7B loan for Syrian projects - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) is joining a group of Gulf lenders to arrange about $7B of debt financing for a Qatari firm's projects in Syria, one of the biggest foreign commitments to the country's reconstruction, according to a media report on Thursday.

The Wall Street bank is working with Qatar National Bank and Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank PJSC on the loan for projects being worked on by a consortium that includes Power International Holding, one of Doha's largest conglomerates, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The loan will help finance the reconstruction of power infrastructure and an airport, the people said. The five-year loan will be guaranteed by QNB, which has the Qatar Investment Authority as its largest shareholder, the article said.

data.title: Crinetics wins new Buy at UBS after recent pullback
data.description:
Crinetics Pharmaceuticals (CRNX) traded higher on Thursday after UBS launched its coverage with a Buy recommendation and a 12-month target of $55, citing an attractive entry point following the stock’s recent decline.

While shares of the San Diego, California-based pharma company have lost more than 10% this year, analyst Ashwani Verma sees opportunity ahead, with multiple catalysts lined up for the next 12-18 months.

Crinetics’ (CRNX) once-daily, oral ACTH antagonist, atumelnant, which is currently undergoing development for congenital adrenal hyperplasia and ACTH-dependent Cushing’s syndrome, is among Verma's most promising bets.

“We believe investors are not giving full credit to atumelnant's pursuit in CAH and/or ACTH-Dependent Cushing's Syndrome,” he wrote.

With a 70% probability of success, the analyst projects $2.0B in risk-adjusted peak sales for atumelnant in CAH alone. Across the company’s four programs, Verma estimates $4.2B in risk-adjusted peak sales compared with $3.0B implied by the stock’s current trading level.

data.title: Mortgage rates hit seven-week low, housing activity estimated to bolster through summer
data.description:
Mortgage rates reached a seven-week low, and housing activity is expected to strengthen through the summer.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.43% as of July 2, down from 6.49% last week and 6.67% in the same period a year ago, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.79%, down from 5.84% a week ago and 5.80% a year ago.

"With rates at a seven-week low and purchase demand continuing to edge higher, it's an encouraging sign as prospective homebuyers respond to modest improvements in affordability," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"Last week's modest decline in mortgage rates helped sustain borrower interest, with home purchase demand up slightly and continuing to outpace last year's levels. Buyers are benefiting from a more balanced housing market as inventory improves and home-price growth moderates in many areas," said Mortgage Bankers Association's CEO Bob Broeksmit.

"If these trends continue, they should bolster housing activity through the summer," said Broeksmit.

data.title: Culp expects break-even to positive adjusted EBITDA in Q1 FY27 as $7M IEPA tariff refunds support debt reduction
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Culp, Inc. (CULP) Q4 fiscal 2026

Management view

  • “The main headline for our fourth quarter performance is the momentum we are building in key areas of our business as we closed out the fiscal year and now move into fiscal 2027,” said (President, CEO & Director Robert Culp), adding, “We are highly encouraged by our ability to achieve overall sales growth for the fourth quarter, both sequentially and year-over-year, in what remains a difficult macro environment.”
  • “Notably, our actions are expected to translate into more than $20 million of annualized savings, efficiencies and other benefits that should meaningfully improve our operating leverage as industry volumes recover,” said (CEO Culp).
  • On product and share positioning in bedding, (CEO Culp) said the bedding business “delivered double-digit sales growth” and that Culp expanded results “through continued share gains with major customers throughout our target market and by driving innovation across our product categories,” highlighting sewn mattress covers as a focus where “our value-add to each mattress unit is higher with sewn covers.”
  • On upholstery, (CEO Culp) described “more challenging headwinds,” but said the company delivered “sequential revenue growth and margin improvement,” and added that “our U.S. upholstery operations are now fully relocated and integrated” into the North Carolina campus, while “our China upholstery operations are also now running more efficiently through a reduced facility footprint and an enhanced outsourcing model.”
  • (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Kenneth Bowling) reported, “Net sales were $51.6 million,” and “gross profit for the quarter was $6.8 million, or 13.2% of sales,” while noting the year-over-year gross profit comparison reflected “a $1.7 million benefit in last year's fourth quarter stemming from a policy change in how we value and reserve for our aged inventory.”

Outlook

  • “Due to continuing macroeconomic uncertainty, a fluid global trade and tariff environment and related matters we continue to see, we are providing only limited forward guidance at this time,” said (CEO Culp).
  • “We expect consolidated sales for the first quarter of fiscal '27 to moderately improve, both sequentially and year-over-year,” said (CEO Culp), and added that restructuring and integration benefits are expected to “drive improving gross profit and lower SG&A expenses.”
  • “We expect … break-even to positive adjusted EBITDA for the first quarter of fiscal year '27, even without the tariff refunds,” said (CEO Culp), while also stating, “We expect our receipt of the $7 million in tariff refunds to, of course, serve to enhance our profitability in the first quarter.”
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s framing on tariffs, Q4 management shifted from pursuing and timing uncertainty to cash received: “during the first quarter of fiscal '27, we have received approximately $7 million in IEPA tariff refunds that we were expecting following recent court rulings,” said (CEO Culp).

Financial results

  • (CFO Bowling) said the company reported “a loss from operations of $1.6 million,” “net loss for the fourth quarter was $2.2 million, or $0.18 per diluted share,” and “adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter was a negative $560,000.”
  • (CFO Bowling) said segment results included bedding sales of “$30.5 million” with bedding gross profit of “$2.7 million or 8.9% of sales,” and upholstery sales of “$21.1 million” with upholstery gross profit of “$4.1 million, or 19.5% of sales.”
  • On balance sheet and cash items, (CFO Bowling) said quarter-end inventory was “$47.5 million,” and added, “we reported $8.3 million in total cash and $19.1 million in outstanding debt… representing a net debt of $10.8 million,” while highlighting liquidity of “approximately $24.2 million.”

Q&A

  • Michael McCormack, Water Tower Research LLC: asked about revenue mix shifting toward bedding, upholstery seasonality, and bedding momentum; (CEO Culp) answered, “we did guide a moderate consolidated increase in revenue for the first quarter,” adding, “I think we should see consistent momentum,” and that near-term “bedding [has] a little more upside short-term than upholstery.”
  • McCormack, Water Tower Research LLC: asked about gross margin differences between segments; (CEO Culp) said, “Upholstery gross margins have been fantastic. Bedding margins are improving… but they're not where they need to be,” and added, “we should expect bedding margins to increase in fiscal '27.”
  • McCormack, Water Tower Research LLC: asked about the cost-savings run rate and whether additional actions could follow; (CEO Culp) said the “$22 million or more than $20 million includes annualized cost savings… and pricing action,” and added, “if we don't see the business growing, then we'll have to take more actions.”
  • McCormack, Water Tower Research LLC: asked how tariff refunds affect interest expense; (CFO Bowling) responded, “We'll focus on that debt first… the higher interest rate is in the U.S.,” adding that this “will significantly reduce our interest expense going forward,” and (CEO Culp) added the company may keep some low-rate China borrowings “just to give us flexibility.”

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts were slightly positive and focused on mix, margins, savings, and tariff-cash implications, with McCormack concluding, “great to see the revenue momentum here.”
  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks and remained steady in Q&A, using confidence language including, “We are quite optimistic that fiscal '27 can represent a turning of the corner for Culp,” and, on Q1, “We expect… break-even to positive adjusted EBITDA.”
  • Versus the prior quarter, management shifted from describing Q3 as “candidly frustrating” to emphasizing Q4 “momentum,” while tariff discussion moved from “we are… taking the steps necessary to be in a position to obtain any available refunds” to refunds received.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Q4 highlighted sequential improvements and a “turning of the corner” narrative; Q3 emphasized demand pressure, a weather disruption (“lose the entire last week of shipping”), and excess inventory markdown pressure.
  • Guidance language stayed limited in both quarters due to macro and tariffs, but Q4 introduced a more specific profitability marker: “break-even to positive adjusted EBITDA” expected in Q1 FY27.
  • Tariff storyline changed from Q3’s “somewhere between $6 million and $7 million” refunds with “unknown” timing to Q4’s “received approximately $7 million” in Q1 FY27.
  • Q&A breadth narrowed in Q4 (single analyst due to connection issues), while Q3 included multiple analysts and investors pressing on breakeven revenue, inventory markdowns, tariff recovery process, and asset monetization.

Risks and concerns

  • (CEO Culp) cited “continuing macroeconomic uncertainty,” “a fluid global trade and tariff environment,” and “rising raw material costs from petrochemical and other supply chain pressures,” adding, “we have also implemented some new pricing actions across both segments.”
  • On upholstery demand sensitivity, (CEO Culp) said residential and hospitality are pressured by “home buying and consumer spending activity,” and “uncertainty stemming from geopolitical conflicts… along with tariffs and inflation.”
  • (CFO Bowling) flagged comparability noise from inventory accounting, saying Q4 year-over-year gross profit was affected by “a $1.7 million benefit” in the prior year from an inventory policy change.

Final takeaway

Management emphasized sequential improvement in Q4 fiscal 2026, positioning more than $20 million of annualized savings as a lever for operating leverage, highlighting bedding share gains and sewn mattress cover expansion, and pointing to Q1 fiscal 2027 expectations for moderate sales improvement and “break-even to positive adjusted EBITDA.” Management also stated that approximately $7 million of IEPA tariff refunds were received in Q1 fiscal 2027 and are expected to reduce debt and improve liquidity, while maintaining limited guidance due to macro and trade uncertainty.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. stocks are mixed after weaker-than-expected payrolls, lowering odds of a rate hike
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes were mixed on Thursday due to a weaker-than-expected June non-farm payrolls report that lowered expectations for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates and a tech trade that continues to struggle.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.2%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.6%, adding to new highs. The heavy-tech Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was last -0.8% as some tech giants such as Tesla (TSLA) and Sandisk (SNDK) declined.

The U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs in June, falling short of the 115,000 increase economists surveyed by Dow Jones had expected. The unemployment rate, however, unexpectedly declined to 4.2%, versus forecasts for it to hold at 4.3%.

Moreover, initial jobless claims fell to 215,000 for the week ended June 27, beating estimates.

Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that the Fed will “chart a new course” on forward guidance, contrasting with his predecessor's practice of indicating which way policymakers were leaning in upcoming meetings.

Odds of an interest rate hike before the end of the year fell, while odds for a 25-basis point cut rose, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Bond yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 2 basis points lower at 4.47%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 5 basis points to 4.13%.

In addition, the U.S. and Iran are conducting negotiations in Doha that focus on the Strait of Hormuz. The Trump administration is trying to convince Iran that it will gain more from a nuclear deal than from tolls in the strait.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $67, while Brent (CO1:COM) was $70 per barrel.

data.title: Two Harbors shareholders vote in favor of sale to CrossCountry
data.description:
Two Harbors Investment (TWO) shareholders voted to approve its sale to CrossCountry Mortgage for $12 a share in cash.

Shareholders approved the acquisition at a vote on Thursday, investors who listened to the webcast of the meeting told Seeking Alpha.

Two Harbors didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment on Thursday.

The latest vote comes after Two Harbors (TWO) last Tuesday adjourned its special meeting of holders until today to provide more time to solicit proxies in favor of the acquisition by CrossCountry.

The struggle for control of Two Harbors (TWO) has dragged on since December, when mortgage wholesaler UWM Holdings (UWMC) agreed to acquire Two Harbors (TWO) in a $1.3B all-stock deal. In March, another bidder, CrossCountry, offered an all-cash proposal. Soon after, TWO terminated its deal with UWMC, calling CrossCountry's proposal "superior." Since then, each side has revised its proposal for the mortgage REIT.

Two Harbors edged higher by 0.6% on Thursday.

data.title: Weak job data handed Bitcoin a lifeline: BTC price reclaims $62K; who else helped?
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) jumped more than ~4% on Thursday to reclaim the ~$62K level as investors reacted positively to weaker-than-expected U.S. jobs data and falling expectations of another Federal Reserve rate hike.

The U.S. economy added just 57K jobs in June, far below estimates of 114K. May payroll figures were also revised lower by 43K jobs. Meanwhile, the unemployment rate fell to 4.2%, slightly better than expectations of 4.3%.

Following the data, traders sharply reduced their bets on a July rate hike. According to the CME FedWatch tool, futures pricing, and markets now see an 82.4% chance that rates will remain unchanged at the Fed's July meeting.

On the other hand, the probability of a 25-basis-point hike dropped to 17.6%, down from 28.9% a day earlier and 32.1% a week ago.

Walter Bloomberg highlighted in a recent X post that traders in short-term interest rate futures were trimming rate hike bets after the weaker jobs report.

The shift in policy expectations boosted sentiment across risk assets, including cryptocurrencies like Ethereum (ETH-USD), XRP (XRP-USD), Solana (SOL-USD), and Hyperliquid (HYPE). 

Bitcoin price responded strongly, climbing back above the key $60K resistance zone, suggesting buyers are regaining momentum after weeks of range-bound trading. Daily trading volume rose 28% to $44.09B, showing renewed buying interest.

Technical indicators also turned more supportive. A bullish MACD crossover, improving volume, and an RSI near 62 propose momentum currently favors buyers.

However, one area remains weak. Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded net outflows of $294.62M on July 1, indicating fear despite the recent recovery.

If expectations for higher interest rates continue to fade, the asset could find additional support in the coming weeks, although the pullback from ~$62K to around $61.7K at the time of writing suggests caution remains.

data.title: Pembina Pipeline to advance C$4.6B data center power project in Alberta
data.description:
Pembina Pipeline (PBA) said Thursday it made a positive final investment decision to advance the Greenlight Electricity Center, a 932 MW gas-fired combined cycle power generation facility to be built in Alberta to serve a major data center development.

Greenlight, a partnership owned 47.5% by Pembina (PBA), 47.5% by Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners, and 5% by Kineticor Asset Management, has obtained a Class 3 level capital cost estimate of ~C$4B, which rises to C$4.6B when including interest during construction and financing costs, resulting in ~C$2.1B net to Pembina.

The facility will supply electricity to the data center under a long-term tolling agreement, with an anticipated in-service date for the project in H2 2030; ~85% of the project's cost has been secured under fixed-price agreements, including an engineering, procurement, and construction contract with a consortium of Aecon Group (AEGXF) and Técnicas Reunidas, with a turbine supply agreement with Siemens Energy (SMEGF).

Pembina (PBA) said it expects the project to generate annual run-rate adjusted EBITDA of ~C$310M net to the company once operational.
